mnemonics.title: Template Declaration
mnemonics.content: Think of TEMPLATE as 'T' + 'EMPLATE' - T stands for Type, the rest is the blueprint (emplate = empty plate to fill)

acronyms.usage: Generic containers, algorithms, and iterators in C++

acronyms.expansion: Substitution Failure Is Not An Error
acronyms.usage: Template resolution principle where invalid overloads are silently skipped
C++ idiom tying resource lifetime to object lifetime
Mechanism to query object types at runtime using typeid

visualTips.visualization: Think of a template like a cookie cutter - one shape (template) can make cookies (instances) of different flavors (types)
Draw a tree: Sequence (vector, list, deque) → Associative (map, set) → Container Adapters (stack, queue)
Iterators are like bookmarks in a book - they mark a position and let you move forward/backward to read elements

analogies.analogy: A recipe template - one recipe format works for baking cakes, cookies, bread by substituting ingredients (types)
A form template - one application form works for different purposes (job application, membership) by filling different details
A universal machine - like a vending machine that takes any product (container) and performs action (algorithm) on it
